chunks.text: 1 Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 70 of 2023 Date Order with Signature of Hon’ble Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.304/2023 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection at Flag ‘A’ 3. For orders on CMA No.305/2023 (Ex.A) 4. For orders on CMA No.306/2023 (S/A) 5. For hearing of main case 17.01.2023 Mr. Imam Bux Mari, Advocate along with petitioners <><><><>..<><><><> 1. Urgency granted. 3. Granted, subject to all just exceptions 2,4&5. At the very outset, learned counsel for the petitioners does not press prayer clause (a) and (c) and states that for the relief sought in the said prayer clauses, the petitioners shall approach the proper forum. Accordingly, this petition to the extent of prayer clauses (a) and (c) stands dismissed as not pressed. Petitioner No.1, namely, Mst. Parveen is present and states that on 09.01.2023 she has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2, namely Sadam Hussain, being sui juris after swearing the affidavit of free will without any coercion, compulsion and pressure. Learned counsel for the petitioners states that since petitioner No.1 has contracted love marriage with petitioner No.2, her mother Mst. Sabhagi, the respondent No.5 being annoyed and lodged a false FIR at Police Station, 2 Sanghar and issuing threats for dire-consequences, hence, they seek protection to their lives. The petition is disposed of along with listed application directing to SHO Police Station, Airport Sukkur within those jurisdiction the petitioners are residing as per their statement to provide necessary protection to the petitioners as and when they approached him in accordance with law.

Sabhagi, the respondent No.5 being annoyed and lodged a false FIR at Police Station, 2 Sanghar and issuing threats for dire-consequences, hence, they seek protection to their lives. The petition is disposed of along with listed application directing to SHO Police Station, Airport Sukkur within those jurisdiction the petitioners are residing as per their statement to provide necessary protection to the petitioners as and when they approached him in accordance with law. Judge Judge ARBROHI
